_metadata.description: MCS sensitivity level definitions shared across all Five Eyes catalogs
_metadata.author: UMRS Project
notes: Sensitivity levels s0-s3 map to SELinux MLS sensitivity labels.
In targeted policy, only s0 is available — all markings collapse to s0.
In MLS policy, s1-s3 provide Bell-LaPadula dominance enforcement.
Level assignments here reflect the MLS canonical mapping. Targeted-mode demotion is a runtime concern.

The sensitivity level alone does NOT imply controlled status. Control is determined by category membership (e.g., c0 = CUI umbrella). A file at s1 with no CUI category is simply unclassified at a higher sensitivity floor.
Commercial or organizational proprietary information may reside at s1-s3 without being CUI or Protected.

s0.name: SystemLow / General
s0.description: Default operating system level. Binaries, libraries, routine unclassified content with no special controls.

s1.name: Unclassified L1
s1.description: First unclassified sensitivity tier above SystemLow. Information here MAY be controlled (via CUI categories) or simply organizational/proprietary. The sensitivity level provides BLP dominance; the categories determine what controls apply.
nations: US CUI (all 72+ categories, when under c0)

s2.name: Unclassified L2
s2.description: Second unclassified sensitivity tier. Higher injury threshold or enhanced handling when categories indicate controlled status.
nations: UK OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE (enforcement mapping — UK policy defines this as a handling caveat, not a classification level)

s3.name: Unclassified L3
s3.description: Highest unclassified sensitivity tier. Approaching the classified boundary. Categories determine whether content is controlled or simply high-sensitivity unclassified.
nations: Australian PROTECTED (distinct classification level in Australian system)
